Foundations — Surfaces & Spacing

The third and final foundations page, completing the Precision Blue system alongside colors.html and typography.html (full trail: context/design-language.html). Radii and the one-light depth model below are locked charter values, rendered live rather than described. The numeric spacing scale in §3 is proposed — it does not yet exist as a token in tokens/apex.json — and is called out as such.

1. Radii — tight, machined · locked

Three radii exist, and only three. Cards and sheets get 10px. Anything nested inside a card — a tap target, a quiet button, a chip — gets 6px. Anything that should read as a pill — a status tag, a "Now" marker — stays full-round at 999px regardless of its content width. Nothing is soft or rounded-for-friendliness; every corner reads as machined.

--r-card10px
--r-nested6px
--r-pill999px
TokenValueRendered on
--r-card10pxCards, sheets, primary buttons — any full surface.
--r-nested6pxControls nested inside a card — tap targets, quiet buttons, chips.
--r-pill999pxPills — status tags, "Now" markers. Full-round at any width.

2. Depth — one light, told twice · locked

Depth comes from a single overhead light, told twice at half strength each: a faint radial halo behind the header lights the room; a shallow vertical card gradient with a brighter top edge lights the objects sitting in it. The scene below renders both halves of that one light at once — this is the complete elevation model, nothing is added beyond it.

Object light
Gradient foot + border-lt top edge

This card's half of the light: a vertical gradient from card-top to card-base, plus a border-lt top edge that catches the light. The halo behind this panel is the room's half — together they sum to depth.

/* halo — room light, sits behind the header on the screen field */
background: radial-gradient(ellipse 90% 45% at 50% -5%,
  var(--halo) 0%, var(--field-mid) 55%, var(--field-base) 100%);

/* card — object light, lit face to gradient foot, brighter top edge */
background: linear-gradient(180deg, var(--card-top) 0%, var(--card-base) 100%);
border: 1px solid var(--border);
border-top-color: var(--border-lt);

Depth sums, it never stacks. These two recipes are the entire elevation model. There is no box-shadow, no filter: drop-shadow, and no glow anywhere in the system — every unit of depth comes from the halo and the card gradient added together once. A shadow or a glow would be a third depth mechanism layered on top of the first two, which the charter's light law forbids.

3. Spacing scale · proposed

This section is proposed, not a locked charter value. It does not yet exist as a token in tokens/apex.json — unlike color and radius, which do. It is being confirmed through wave-1 screens: as real screens ship against it without needing a value outside this set, the scale graduates to locked. Until then, treat every number below as a working default, not a contract.

A 4-pt base scale — six steps, rendered at true size below.

4
8
12
16
20
24
ValueApplies to
20pxScreen horizontal padding — left/right inset for all screen content.
14px × 16pxCard padding — vertical 14, horizontal 16. The one exception to the 4-pt grid (14 isn't a multiple of 4); already shipped this way in templates/screen-template.html and both prior foundations pages, not introduced here.
12pxInter-card gap — vertical space between stacked cards on a screen.
24pxSection gap — vertical space between named groups of cards.
48pxSet-row min-height — minimum row height for a logged or prescribed set line.
≥44pxTap target — minimum touch target for any interactive control (Apple HIG floor).

The two touch-critical minimums, rendered at true size:

set-row · 48
44

4. Hairlines · locked

For surfaces — cards, dividers, sheets — the rule is exact: every border is 1px solid var(--border); var(--border-lt) is only ever a top-edge override, never a full outline. This is the entire mechanism behind the card in §2 reading as machined rather than flat — three edges recede into border, the top edge alone catches the light.

--border-lt --border --border --border

The same hairline, used flat, separates rows inside a surface — no top-edge treatment, just a 1px divider:

Set 182.5kg × 6
Set 282.5kg × 6
Set 382.5kg × 6

Open, not resolved here: templates/screen-template.html currently sets --border-lt as the full outline — all four edges — on small interactive controls (.tap, .pill, .btn-quiet), which is in tension with the top-edge-only rule stated above for surfaces. This page states the surface rule as it stands today; whether interactive controls should follow the same top-edge-only law or keep their current full-outline treatment is a pending wave-1 decision for Ben, not resolved either way by this page.
